title: Pentagon strikes deals with 7 Big Tech companies after shunning Anthropic
author: Sigma Capital
metaDescription: The Pentagon has formed agreements with seven major tech firms, excluding Anthropic, after the latter refused to allow military use of its AI for all lawful pur
excerpt: The Pentagon has formed agreements with seven major tech firms, excluding Anthropic, after the latter refused to allow military use of its AI for all lawful purposes. This decision followed a lawsuit by Anthropic against the administration. The new deals focus on AI applications for defense.

content.introduction: The Pentagon has formed agreements with seven major tech firms, excluding Anthropic, after the latter refused to allow military use of its AI for all lawful purposes. This decision followed a lawsuit by Anthropic against the administration. The new deals focus on AI applications for defense.
body: * [Pentagon strikes deals with 7 Big Tech companies after shunning Anthropic](/2026/05/01/tech/pentagon-ai-anthropic). # Pentagon strikes deals with 7 Big Tech companies after shunning Anthropic. The Pentagon has existing AI contracts with several companies, including Palantir and [OpenAI](https://www.cnn.com/2026/02/27/tech/openai-pentagon-deal-ai-systems). But President Donald Trump announced the administration would [sever ties](https://www.cnn.com/2026/02/27/tech/anthropic-pentagon-deadline) with the company after Anthropic refused to back down on terms that would allow the military to use Claude for “all lawful purposes,” including autonomous weapons and mass surveillance. Anthropic sued the Trump administration in response, and a federal judge in California last month [blocked](https://www.cnn.com/2026/03/26/business/anthropic-pentagon-injunction-supply-chain-risk) the government’s effort. But Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei visited the White House last month for a meeting with Chief of Staff Susie Wiles after Anthropic unveiled its [Mythos tool](https://www.cnn.com/2026/04/07/tech/anthropic-claude-mythos-preview-cybersecurity) that can identify cybersecurity threats – but also present a roadmap for hackers to attack companies or the government.
Hadas Gold (@Hadas_Gold). 6 likes. Pentagon strikes deals with 7 Big Tech companies after shunning Anthropic.
